notes: This is an 'easy' code OCR test: well-formatted Python with no unusual characters, sufficient contrast, and standard syntax-highlighted dark theme. All 15 model variants produced near-perfect verbatim transcriptions of the code block, capturing all 13 key code elements (imports, app init, class def, three fields with types, decorator, async function signature, comment, two db operations, return statement). No hallucinated imports or fabricated code were observed in any response. Two responses had minor metadata (color-legend) inconsistencies that did NOT affect the code transcription: qwen3-vl-8b|think wrote the route as 'users' (without slash) in the color description (code block correct), and qwen3.6-35b-a3b|nothink mislabeled the comment as a string literal in its color legend (code block correct). These minor errors were not severe enough to warrant dropping below score 9. The think vs nothink distinction was negligible here — both configurations of qwen3.5-4b and qwen3.5-9b produced identical-quality code. qwen3.6-27b|nothink had a truncated response tail but the code block was complete. Overall, this test image does not effectively differentiate model quality — every variant excels.
